attractions.name: 1933 Lounge by St. Elmo (1933 Lounge)
attractions.description: Tucked away on the second floor above the iconic St. Elmo Steak House, 1933 Lounge is a speakeasy-style retreat that extends the legendary St. Elmo experience. It features an intimate, upscale atmosphere with a restored 1800s vintage bar, offering classic cocktails and a refined bar menu for guests seeking a sophisticated escape (1933 Lounge, Visit Indy).
attractions.address: 127 S. Illinois St., Indianapolis, IN 46225 (1933 Lounge)
highlights: World-Famous St. Elmo Shrimp Cocktail with spicy signature sauce (1933 Lounge)
Speakeasy-style atmosphere with a restored 1800s vintage Brunswick bar (Visit Indy)
Signature cocktails like the "Elmo Cola" with cherry-vanilla whiskey (1933 Lounge)
Selection of premium sliders including Filet, Fried Shrimp, and Wagyu with Brie (1933 Lounge)
Intimate lounge seating with fireplaces and a dapper, historic aesthetic (Indianapolis Monthly)
Exclusive environment for patrons 21 and over (1933 Lounge)

attractions.name: 450 North Brewing Company
attractions.description: 450 North Brewing Company is a family-owned craft brewery and restaurant situated on the Simmons family farm in Columbus, Indiana. Since its opening in 2012, the brewery has gained national recognition for its innovative "Slushy" series of smoothie sours and its signature brick oven-fired pizzas. The location uniquely combines a brewery, the Simmons Winery, and 450 North Spirits, offering visitors a comprehensive craft beverage experience in a scenic rural setting. 450 North Brewing, Artisan Beverage Group
highlights: Signature "Slushy" series of heavily fruited smoothie sours and pastry sours.
Handcrafted, brick oven-fired pizzas including local favorites like the Chicken Bacon Ranch.
On-site Simmons Winery and 450 North Spirits distillery for wine and spirit tastings.
Spacious outdoor patio seating overlooking the surrounding farm and rural landscape.
Hosts the annual Corn Maze Beer Fest, a major regional craft beer event.
Weekly dining specials such as "Tenderloin Tuesday" and "Happy Hour Fridays."

attractions.name: Abe Martin Lodge at Brown County State Park (Official Website)
attractions.description: The Abe Martin Lodge is a historic inn nestled within the picturesque hills of Indiana’s largest state park, Brown County State Park. Built in 1932 using native stone and oak timbers, it offers a blend of rustic charm and modern amenities, including a full-service restaurant and a massive indoor aquatic center. (Official Website)
attractions.address: 1405 State Road 46 West, Nashville, IN 47448 (Official Website)
highlights: 12,000 square foot indoor aquatic center with water slides and a lazy river (Brown County State Park)
Historic lodge built in 1932 with native stone and hand-hewn timbers (Our Brown County)
Little Gem Restaurant serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner daily (Official Website)
Over 80 lodge rooms and a variety of rustic and family cabins (Official Website)
Access to nearly 16,000 acres of parkland with extensive hiking and biking trails (Wikipedia)
On-site banquet and conference facilities for weddings and corporate events (WeddingWire)

attractions.name: Ale Emporium (Castleton)
attractions.description: Ale Emporium is a family-run sports pub and restaurant that has been an Indianapolis landmark since opening its doors in 1982. It is renowned for its 'Honest Pizza' and world-famous 'Hermanaki Wings,' serving as a popular local gathering spot for live music, sports viewing, and fresh food. The Castleton location is strictly for guests aged 21 and over, offering a high-energy atmosphere with nearly 200 TVs and 90 draft lines.
highlights: Home of the World Famous Hermanaki® Wings and Honest Pizza.
Features over 193 TVs and 90 draft lines for a premier sports viewing experience.
Offers a large outdoor patio and private party room availability.
Live music every weekend featuring local bands and artists.
Voted 'Best Wings' and 'Best Sports Bar' by local readers and critics.
The original 1982 location serves as a significant community supporter and landmark.

attractions.name: Architecture Highlights Tour (via Columbus Area Visitors Center)
attractions.description: The Architecture Highlights Tour is a guided shuttle experience that explores dozens of significant modernist sites in Columbus, Indiana. Led by expert guides, the tour provides an in-depth introduction to the city's world-renowned architectural heritage, featuring works by masters like Eero Saarinen, I.M. Pei, and Robert Venturi.
highlights: Guided shuttle tour of over 70 significant modernist buildings and public art sites
Close-up interior looks at two landmark sites, typically including First Christian Church
Expert commentary on the history of 'The Athens of the Prairie' and the Cummins Foundation Architecture Program
Starting point at the historic Columbus Visitors Center, featuring Dale Chihuly glass art
Viewing of iconic structures by Pritzker Prize-winning architects like I.M. Pei and Richard Meier
Access to the city's seven National Historic Landmarks during the comprehensive 2-hour circuit

attractions.name: Bazbeaux Pizza
attractions.description: Established in 1986, Bazbeaux Pizza is a legendary Indianapolis institution famous for its innovative gourmet thin-crust pizzas and extensive selection of over 50 unique toppings. The restaurant helped anchor the downtown Arts District and has consistently been recognized as one of the city's top pizza destinations for decades (Visit Indy, Dan vs. Food).
highlights: Famous 'Pizza Alla Quattro Formaggio' featuring five cheeses and bacon (Bazbeaux Menu)
Over 50 unique toppings including exotic options like cajun shrimp and lump crab (Indy Pizza Blog)
Long-standing 'Best Pizza in Indianapolis' award winner since 1986 (Random Ripplings)
Freshly prepared ingredients with white and wheat crust options (Yelp)
Vibrant outdoor patio seating and a relaxed neighborhood atmosphere (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Bean Blossom Covered Bridge
attractions.description: The Bean Blossom Covered Bridge is a historic single-lane Howe through truss bridge built in 1880. It is the oldest covered bridge in Indiana still standing in its original location and is renowned for its picturesque setting, making it one of the most photographed and painted bridges in the state. (Tripadvisor, Wikipedia)
highlights: Oldest covered bridge in Indiana still at its original site (Tripadvisor)
Built in 1880 by Civil War veteran Captain Joseph Balsley (YouTube)
One of only three Howe Single Through Truss bridges remaining in existence (Brown County Tourism)
Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 2022 (Wikipedia)
Iconic subject for regional artists and photographers (IndyStar)
Spans the scenic Bean Blossom Creek in a deep valley setting (Bridges and Tunnels)

attractions.name: Bluebeard
attractions.description: Bluebeard is an award-winning farm-to-table restaurant and bar located in a renovated 1924 factory warehouse in downtown Indianapolis (Bluebeard Indy). Named after a novel by Indianapolis native Kurt Vonnegut, it specializes in contemporary American cuisine featuring daily rotating menus inspired by the freshest local ingredients (Visit Indy).
highlights: James Beard Award semi-finalist for Best New Restaurant and Outstanding Hospitality (Bluebeard Indy)
Features hearth-baked European-style bread from the on-site Amelia's Bakery (Bluebeard Indy)
Daily rotating menu highlighting local farmers and seasonal produce (Bluebeard Indy)
Located in the historic Holy Rosary neighborhood within a repurposed industrial space (Tripadvisor)
Extensive beverage program including house-curated wines and inventive cocktails (Bluebeard Indy)
Named in honor of local literary icon Kurt Vonnegut (Wikipedia)

attractions.name: BODHI (Craft Bar + Thai Bistro) Official Website
attractions.description: BODHI is a 21+ Thai craft cocktail bar and bistro that blends authentic Thai cooking with modern seasonal cocktails in a cozy, industrial-chic setting. The family-owned establishment features recipes passed down through three generations of Thai women, offering a menu that highlights both traditional and contemporary flavors. Its lush outdoor patio serves as an enchanting urban oasis on the northeast end of Indianapolis's vibrant Mass Ave. Official Website, IndyStar
attractions.address: 922 Massachusetts Avenue, Indianapolis, IN 46202 Official Website, Visit Indy
attractions.days_open: Monday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday Official Website
highlights: Named one of USA TODAY's 2025 Restaurants of the Year IndyStar
Menu inspired by three generations of Thai women Official Website
Creative cocktail program featuring Thai ingredients like galangal, lemongrass, and Thai chili syrup Visit Indy
Award-winning dishes including braised beef massaman curry and som tum (papaya salad) IndyStar
Atmospheric industrial-chic interior complemented by a lush outdoor patio Official Website

attractions.name: Brickyard 400 presented by PPG
attractions.description: The Brickyard 400 is a premier NASCAR Cup Series race held annually on the historic 2.5-mile oval at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway. As one of NASCAR's "Crown Jewel" events, it features top stock car drivers competing for the honor of "kissing the bricks" at the finish line. The race weekend serves as an American summer classic, blending high-speed competition with deep-rooted motorsports tradition. Indianapolis Motor Speedway, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: The Museum is open 363 days a year (daily except Thanksgiving and Christmas). The 2026 Brickyard Weekend events are scheduled for Friday, July 24 through Sunday, July 26. Indianapolis Motor Speedway, IMS Museum
highlights: Kissing the Bricks: A legendary tradition where the winning driver and team kiss the yard of bricks at the start/finish line.
NASCAR Hauler Parade: A festive event on Main Street in Speedway where fans watch the colorful team haulers arrive.
IMS Museum: A world-class museum on the grounds featuring historic race cars and extensive motorsports exhibits.
Fan Midway: An interactive area for fans of all ages featuring driver appearances, simulators, and games.
Gasoline Alley: The famous garage area where fans can get a close-up look at the cars and teams preparing for the race.
Pre-Race Track Walk: An opportunity for fans to walk the historic 2.5-mile oval before the green flag drops.

attractions.name: Brickyard Crossing Golf Course
attractions.description: Brickyard Crossing is a world-renowned championship golf course located on the grounds of the iconic Indianapolis Motor Speedway. Designed by legendary architect Pete Dye, the 18-hole layout features 14 holes adjacent to the racetrack's backstretch and four holes uniquely situated within the speedway's famous 2.5-mile oval infield. Source, Source
attractions.days_open: Daily during the golf season (typically late March/April to October/November), though closed to the public on major race days (e.g., Indianapolis 500). Source, Source
highlights: Four holes (7-10) located entirely within the infield of the Indianapolis Motor Speedway. Source
Redesigned by Hall of Fame architect Pete Dye in 1993. Source
Course features signature railroad ties and concrete creek banks repurposed from the track's former walls. Source
Hosted professional events for the PGA, LPGA, and PGA Champions Tour. Source
Historical association with The Beatles, who famously posed for photos on the practice green during their 1964 tour. Source
Consistently ranked among the top 100 public golf courses in the United States by Golf Digest and Golfweek. Source

attractions.name: Broad Ripple Village
attractions.description: Broad Ripple Village is a vibrant neighborhood and designated cultural district in Indianapolis known for its eclectic mix of shops, restaurants, and nightlife. It features a unique blend of urban amenities and outdoor recreation, with the Monon Trail and White River providing popular scenic escapes for residents and visitors alike.
highlights: Monon Trail (26-mile rail-to-trail greenway)
Indianapolis Art Center and its ArtsPark
Broad Ripple Park and Family Center
Indiana Central Canal (scenic towpath)
Vibrant nightlife and live music venues (e.g., The Vogue)
Broad Ripple Farmers Market (Saturdays)

attractions.name: Brown County State Park
attractions.description: Brown County State Park, nicknamed the 'Little Smokies' for its resemblance to the Great Smoky Mountains, is Indiana's largest state park, spanning nearly 16,000 acres. It features rugged hills, scenic vistas, and nearly 20 miles of tree-lined roads famous for their autumn foliage. Indiana DNR
attractions.address: 1801 State Road 46 E, Nashville, IN 47448 (North Gate); 1405 State Road 46 W, Nashville, IN 47448 (West Gate)
highlights: Climb the 90-foot Fire Tower for panoramic views of the 'Hills o' Brown.'
See the rare, state-endangered Yellowwood tree on Trail 5.
Visit the Nature Center to view a rare timber rattlesnake.
Enjoy the indoor water park at the historic Abe Martin Lodge.
Explore nearly 20 miles of mountain bike trails, ranked among the best on the continent.
Watch sunrises or sunsets from iconic vistas like Hesitation Point.

attractions.name: Cafe Patachou (Meridian Kessler Flagship)
attractions.description: Long described as a “student union for adults,” Cafe Patachou is an award-winning flagship cafe in Indianapolis that has been a community hub since 1989. It offers a world-class all-day breakfast and lunch experience featuring scratch-made dishes, premium local ingredients, and a vibrant, welcoming atmosphere.
highlights: Legendary thick-sliced Cinnamon Toast drenched in butter and cinnamon sugar
World-class French-style omelettes made with organic, pasture-raised eggs
Iconic 'Broken Yolk' breakfast sandwiches on toasted mild sourdough
Vibrant, neighborhood-centric 'student union for adults' atmosphere
Commitment to sourcing local, sustainable, and often organic ingredients
Award-winning coffee bar featuring locally roasted Hubbard & Cravens coffee

attractions.name: Central Canal Walk (Downtown Canal)
attractions.description: The Downtown Canal is a three-mile urban promenade and scenic waterway that serves as a central artery for outdoor activities in Indianapolis. Originally part of the 19th-century Indiana Central Canal, it has been transformed into a vibrant cultural district featuring walkways, public art, and recreational opportunities like pedal boating and gondola rides. (Visit Indy), (Homes.com)
attractions.days_open: 7 days a week, 365 days a year (Indianapolis Cultural Trail)
highlights: Walk, run, or bike along the 3-mile loop with scenic skyline views (Indianapolis Cultural Trail)
Rent a pedal boat, kayak, or take a romantic gondola ride (Visit Indy)
Explore the Colts Canal Playspace and Pawp-Up Pup Park (Indianapolis Cultural Trail)
View public art murals and historic memorials like the USS Indianapolis and Medal of Honor Memorials (Indianapolis Cultural Trail)
Dine at waterside cafes and restaurants along the promenade (Indianapolis Cultural Trail)

attractions.name: Chilly Water Brewing Company
attractions.description: Chilly Water Brewing Company is an urban brewpub founded in 2014, known for providing high-quality, true-to-style ales and lagers in a laid-back setting. The brewery integrates a love for music into its brand, featuring beer names inspired by songs and hosting regular live performances by local and regional artists. Along with a rotating tap list of 12 beers, it offers elevated casual bites for lunch and dinner.
highlights: Offers a rotating tap list of 12 craft beers including the 'Built to Last Pilsner' and 'Blood on the Tracks IPA'.
Regularly hosts live music performances, including Friday night sets and monthly Bluegrass and Jazz jam sessions.
Known for its elevated casual food menu, featuring the popular 'Vinny' sandwich and various vegetarian/vegan options.
Features a music-themed atmosphere where beer names and branding are inspired by the founders' passion for music.
Located along the Indianapolis Cultural Trail in the historic Fletcher Place neighborhood.
Multiple taproom locations including the Downtown brewpub, a Butler University area taproom, and a Greenwood site.

attractions.name: Columbus Area Visitors Center
attractions.description: The Columbus Area Visitors Center serves as the gateway to the city's world-renowned modernist architecture and public art. It offers a variety of guided tours, self-guided resources, and features unique art installations, including works by Dale Chihuly. Visit Columbus Indiana
attractions.days_open: Monday – Saturday (Closed Sundays and specific holidays; closed Mondays in January and February) Visit Columbus Indiana
highlights: Home to Dale Chihuly's 'Yellow Neon Chandelier' and 'Persians' glass installations. Visit Columbus Indiana
Official starting point for guided shuttle tours of the city's architectural landmarks. Visit Columbus Indiana
Exclusive provider of guided tours for the iconic National Historic Landmark, Miller House and Garden. Visit Columbus Indiana
Features a curated gift shop with local artisan goods and architecture-themed souvenirs. Visit Columbus Indiana
Provides free orientation videos and comprehensive maps for self-guided explorations. Visit Columbus Indiana

attractions.name: Conner Prairie Museum Inc. (GuideStar)
attractions.description: Conner Prairie is a nationally acclaimed living history museum and Smithsonian Affiliate that spans over 1,000 wooded acres in Fishers, Indiana. It provides immersive, interactive experiences across several historically themed indoor and outdoor areas, including a 19th-century pioneer village and a tethered balloon voyage (Visit Indy). The museum inspires curiosity by combining history and art with science, technology, engineering, and math (LinkedIn).
attractions.days_open: Tuesday through Sunday (April–October); Thursday through Sunday (November–March) (Visit Indy)
highlights: 1836 Prairietown: A recreated pioneer community with first-person interpreters (Official Website).
1859 Balloon Voyage: A tethered helium balloon that rises 377 feet into the air (Wikipedia).
Promised Land as Proving Ground: An exhibit exploring the influence of African culture on American culture (Visit Indy).
Treetop Outpost: A four-story treehouse and nature-focused activity center (Official Website).
Lenape Indian Camp: A recreation of a native Lenape camp focused on fur trading and lifeways (Visit Indy).
Animal Encounters: A working barn where guests can interact with heritage breed livestock (Official Website).

attractions.name: Crown Hill Cemetery
attractions.description: Crown Hill Cemetery is a historic "rural cemetery" and the third-largest non-government cemetery in the United States, spanning 555 acres of rolling hills and trees. Founded in 1863 during the Civil War, it serves as a serene sanctuary for wildlife, a Level II accredited arboretum, and a final resting place for over 250,000 individuals, including many notable political and cultural figures. (Crown Hill Foundation, Tripadvisor)
attractions.days_open: Every day, including holidays (Crown Hill Foundation)
highlights: The Crown: The highest point in Indianapolis, featuring the grave of poet James Whitcomb Riley and a 360-degree panoramic view of the city skyline. (Crown Hill Foundation)
Gothic Gate and Waiting Station: An iconic 1885 limestone entrance at 34th Street, listed on the National Register of Historic Places. (Crown Hill Foundation)
National Cemeteries: Contains both a Union National Cemetery and a Confederate Plot, reflecting its origins during the Civil War. (Crown Hill Foundation)
Level II Arboretum: Home to thousands of trees and several rare species, providing a lush urban oasis for visitors and wildlife. (Crown Hill Foundation)
Notable Burials: Final resting place of President Benjamin Harrison, several U.S. Vice Presidents, and industrialist Eli Lilly. (Crown Hill Foundation)

attractions.name: Eagle Creek Park
attractions.description: Eagle Creek Park is one of the nation's 10 largest municipal parks, encompassing 3,900 acres of land and 1,400 acres of water. It serves as a major nature reserve in Indianapolis, offering extensive opportunities for hiking, birding, and water-based recreation on the Eagle Creek Reservoir. Eagle Creek Park Foundation, Wikipedia
highlights: Over 30 miles of well-maintained hiking and multi-use trails
Eagle Creek Reservoir for boating, fishing, and rowing activities
Earth Discovery Center and Ornithology Center for nature education
Go Ape Treetop Adventure course and Zip Line adventure
Swimming beach, marina, and 36-hole golf course facilities
Designated 'Important Bird Area' by the National Audubon Society

attractions.name: Eagle Creek Park Ornithology Center (Indy Parks)
attractions.description: The Ornithology Center is a unique facility within Eagle Creek Park dedicated to the study of birds and wildlife conservation. It features interactive exhibits, observation decks overlooking a bird sanctuary, and is home to 'Raptor Ambassadors' used for educational programming (Indy Parks, Eagle Creek Park Foundation).
highlights: Home to non-releasable 'Raptor Ambassadors' (birds of prey) used for public education (Indy Parks)
Three observation decks overlooking a bird sanctuary, reservoir, and hummingbird garden (Eagle Creek Park Foundation)
800-square-foot exhibit hall featuring roughly 100 taxidermy birds in four distinct Indiana habitats (Eagle Creek Park Foundation)
Interactive 'Outdoor Migration Game' designed to teach children about the challenges of bird migration (Eagle Creek Park Foundation)
Regular Sunday Morning Bird Walks led by the Amos Butler Audubon Society (Eagle Creek Park Foundation)
Extensive hands-on kiosks and displays focusing on bird calls and species identification (Eagle Creek Park Foundation)

attractions.name: Eiteljorg Museum of American Indians and Western Art
attractions.description: The Eiteljorg Museum of American Indians and Western Art is a premier cultural institution located in Indianapolis's White River State Park. It houses an extensive collection of visual arts by indigenous peoples of the Americas alongside significant Western American paintings and sculptures. The museum is renowned for its world-class contemporary Native art collection and offers diverse exhibitions and programs that explore the history and cultures of the American West. Eiteljorg Official Site, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Daily except for Thanksgiving, Christmas, and New Year’s Day Eiteljorg Hours
highlights: Native American Galleries featuring the "Expressions of Life" exhibition
The Gund Gallery of Western Art containing works by Frederic Remington and Charles Russell
Extensive collection of Contemporary Native Art, including the Fellowship program works
Jingle Rails: A seasonal holiday model train exhibit featuring miniature landmarks
The R.B. Annis Western Family Experience, a hands-on discovery center for all ages
The Annual Indian Market and Festival celebrating Native arts and cultures

attractions.name: Everwise Amphitheater at White River State Park
attractions.description: Everwise Amphitheater at White River State Park is a premier 6,000-capacity outdoor concert venue located in downtown Indianapolis. The facility features a state-of-the-art permanent stage with a distinctive artistic canopy covering 2,500 reserved seats and a spacious lawn for 3,500 general admission guests. White River State Park, Live Nation
attractions.days_open: Open daily for park grounds (5:00 AM – 11:00 PM); Box Office open only on show days (12:00 PM – 8:00 PM). White River State Park, Everwise FAQs
attractions.phone: (317) 776-8161 (Venue) / (317) 233-2434 (Park Office) Everwise FAQs, White River State Park
highlights: Award-winning architectural design with a permanent canopy White River State Park
Total capacity of 6,000 guests (2,500 reserved, 3,500 lawn) Everwise Amphitheater
Intimate VIP Club with private bar and covered lounge Everwise Upgrades
Cashless venue supporting all major credit cards and mobile payments Everwise FAQs
Breathtaking views of the Indianapolis skyline and White River Visit Indy

attractions.name: Fat Dan's Chicago-Style Deli
attractions.description: Fat Dan's is a real deal, authentic Chicago-style deli and pub featuring "smokin' good" Chicago comfort food alongside a selection of craft beers and wines. Voted one of the best spots for chicken wings in Indianapolis, this family-owned establishment prides itself on a "no bull, no chain, no gimmicks" approach to dining. (Official Website, Visit Indy)
highlights: Authentic Chicago-style specialties like Italian Beef and Chicago Dogs
Award-winning slow-smoked chicken wings
Wide variety of local and regional craft beers on tap
Unique sandwiches such as the "Griffin\
and

attractions.name: Fernando's Mexican and Brazilian Cuisine
attractions.description: Fernando's Mexican and Brazilian Cuisine blends the vibrant flavors of Mexico and Brazil to create a unique culinary experience in Indianapolis. Their dishes are crafted with passion and authenticity, taking diners on a journey through the rich traditions of both cultures. The menu features a variety of authentic items ranging from Brazil's national dish, feijoada, to Mexican favorites like tacos and enchiladas.
attractions.address: 834 East 64th Street, Indianapolis, IN 46220; 888 Massachusetts Ave, Indianapolis, IN 46204
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday
highlights: Feijoada (Brazil's national dish) - a stew of black beans, pork belly, and sausage.
Sinaloa Tacos - crispy tortillas topped with shrimp and poblano peppers.
Picanha - premium top sirloin served with farofa, vinaigrette, rice, and beans.
Coxinha - traditional Brazilian chicken croquettes.
Caipirinha Flights - a classic Brazilian cocktail offered in multiple fruit flavors.
Burripple - the restaurant's signature burrito.

attractions.name: Flat12 Bierwerks
attractions.description: Flat12 Bierwerks was a production craft brewery and popular taproom located in the Holy Cross neighborhood of Indianapolis. Named after a 12-cylinder racing engine, the brewery was known for its "uncommonly distinct" craft beers, including its flagship Half Cycle IPA and the acclaimed Pinko! Russian Imperial Stout. The facility featured a rustic lounge atmosphere and a dog-friendly outdoor biergarten with a fire pit. Downtown Indy, Indiana on Tap
highlights: Automotive-themed branding inspired by Indianapolis racing history IBJ
Home to "Hoagies & Hops," an in-house Philly-style sandwich shop Tripadvisor
Dog-friendly outdoor biergarten featuring a communal fire pit Yelp
Famous for the annual release of "Pinko!" Russian Imperial Stout CraftBeer.com
Regular host of community events, including live music and the Winter Wood barrel-aged beer festival Indiana on Tap

attractions.name: Fletcher Place, Fletcher Place Official Site
attractions.description: Fletcher Place is a vibrant, historic neighborhood and culinary hotspot located just southeast of downtown Indianapolis. Named after prominent banker Calvin Fletcher, the district is celebrated for its well-preserved 19th-century architecture, including Italianate and Gothic Revival homes, and its seamless connection to the city via the Indianapolis Cultural Trail. Fletcher Place Official Site, Life In Indy
attractions.address: 555 Lord Street, Indianapolis, IN 46202 (Neighborhood Association), Fletcher Place Neighborhood Association
highlights: Indianapolis Cultural Trail: An 8-mile urban bike and pedestrian path that runs through the heart of the neighborhood.
Edna Balz Lacy Park: A two-acre community hub featuring a playground, bocce courts, and a sand volleyball court.
Award-Winning Dining: Home to nationally acclaimed restaurants like Bluebeard, Milktooth, and the historic Iaria’s Italian Restaurant.
Annual Italian Street Festival: A major June event at Holy Rosary Catholic Church featuring live music and authentic Italian cuisine.
Historic Architecture: A federally recognized historic district with stunning 19th-century residential structures.
The Great Neighborhood Glow Up: Vibrant street murals and public art installations located at key intersections.

attractions.name: Fountain Square Neighborhood
attractions.description: Fountain Square is a vibrant, eclectic neighborhood located just 1.5 miles southeast of downtown Indianapolis, recognized as the city's first commercial historic district. It is widely celebrated for its thriving arts scene, one-of-a-kind vintage shops, and a dense concentration of independent restaurants and live music venues. Visit Indy, Life In Indy
attractions.address: Intersection of Virginia Ave, Shelby St, and Prospect St (1043 Virginia Ave, Ste 4)
highlights: Historic Fountain Square Theatre Building featuring retro duckpin bowling
Direct connection to the 8-mile Indianapolis Cultural Trail for cycling and walking
The Murphy Arts Center, home to dozens of working artists and 'First Friday' gallery tours
An extensive collection of street murals and public art throughout the district
Vibrant nightlife and dining scene with local breweries and rooftop bars
The iconic Lady Spray Fountain at the neighborhood's central intersection

attractions.name: Futuro Pizza
attractions.description: Futuro is a handcrafted pizza joint located in the Holy Cross neighborhood of Indianapolis, specializing in Detroit-style and Chicago Tavern-style pizzas. Born during the COVID-19 pandemic, it offers a focus on fresh, housemade ingredients, including vegan and gluten-free options, alongside a selection of craft beer and cider.
highlights: Specializes in Detroit-style and Chicago Tavern-style pizza
Handmade dough prepared daily from scratch
Extensive vegan and gluten-free menu options available
Offers local craft beers and ciders on tap
Features a unique two-story angular building with garage doors and outdoor seating
Counter-service model with text notifications when orders are ready

attractions.name: Gainbridge Fieldhouse
attractions.description: Gainbridge Fieldhouse is a premier multi-purpose arena that serves as the home for the NBA's Indiana Pacers and the WNBA's Indiana Fever. Opened in 1999, the venue is celebrated for its retro-style architecture that pays homage to Indiana's rich basketball history while offering modern, state-of-the-art amenities. It anchors the downtown Indianapolis area, hosting a wide range of events including concerts, college tournaments, and community gatherings. Official Website
attractions.days_open: Open daily for scheduled events; Box Office is typically open Monday through Friday. Gainbridge Fieldhouse A-Z Guide
highlights: Home of the Indiana Pacers (NBA) and Indiana Fever (WNBA).
Retro-style fieldhouse architecture designed to capture the essence of Indiana basketball.
Host to major events like the Big Ten Basketball Tournaments and the NCAA Women's Final Four.
Morris Bicentennial Plaza, featuring an outdoor basketball court that converts to an ice rink in winter.
The 'Sphere,' a reflective sculpture with an interior video screen located in the plaza.
Extensive $360 million renovations completed to modernize seating, clubs, and fan experiences.

attractions.name: Garfield Park
attractions.description: Garfield Park is the oldest city park in Indianapolis, spanning over 122 acres of rolling landscape and historic architecture. It is best known for its 10,000-square-foot tropical Conservatory and formal Sunken Gardens, which were designed by renowned landscape architect George Kessler. The park serves as a cultural hub for the near Southside, offering a blend of natural beauty, visual arts, and community recreation.
attractions.days_open: Conservatory & Sunken Garden: Tuesday–Sunday; Park Grounds: Daily. Official Site
highlights: Garfield Park Conservatory: A 10,000-square-foot tropical rainforest featuring waterfalls and exotic plants.
Sunken Gardens: Historic formal gardens with tiered fountains and seasonal floral displays.
Garfield Park Arts Center: A community art space with galleries, classrooms, and a performing arts theater.
MacAllister Center for the Performing Arts: An outdoor amphitheater hosting concerts and theater productions.
Blake’s Garden: A whimsical children's garden designed for nature exploration and imaginative play.
Garfield Park Aquatic Center: A seasonal outdoor facility with multiple pools and water slides.

attractions.name: Garfield Park Conservatory and Sunken Gardens
attractions.description: Garfield Park Conservatory is a 10,000-square-foot tropical oasis featuring hundreds of different plants from the world's tropics, including a 15-foot granite waterfall. Adjacent to it, the three-acre Sunken Garden offers European-style formal landscapes with seasonal floral displays and classical fountains. Official Site, Wikipedia)
highlights: 10,000-square-foot tropical Conservatory with exotic plants and a waterfall
Three-acre European-style formal Sunken Gardens with active fountains
Seasonal floral displays including spring tulips and summer annuals
Blake's Garden, a refurbished outdoor space for nature exploration and play
Historical architecture designed by George Kessler, dating back to 1916
Regular community programs like Jungle Tales and Master Gardener tours

attractions.name: Go Ape Zipline & Adventure Park (Indianapolis) Go Ape Official Website
attractions.category: Entertainment / Adventure Park Go Ape Official Website
attractions.description: Go Ape Zipline & Adventure Park is an interactive outdoor experience set within the lush canopy of Eagle Creek Park. The park features a 2-3 hour treetop course with 40+ obstacles, including suspended bridges and Tarzan swings, plus five long ziplines with views of the reservoir. It also offers a ground-based Forest EscAPE outdoor escape room and professional team-building programs Go Ape Official Website, Visit Indy.
attractions.address: 5855 Delong Road, Indianapolis, IN 46254 Go Ape Official Website
attractions.days_open: Seasonal, typically from March through November; operating days vary by month Go Ape Official Website, Eagle Creek Park Foundation
highlights: Five epic ziplines, including a 305-foot crossing Go Ape Official Website
More than 40 unique treetop obstacles and crossings Go Ape Official Website
Iconic Tarzan swings for a heart-pounding leap Go Ape Official Website
Forest EscAPE: A private, 60-minute outdoor escape room mission Go Ape Official Website
Primal Dynamix team-building and leadership development programs Go Ape Official Website
Scenic views over the 1,400-acre Eagle Creek Reservoir Go Ape Official Website

attractions.name: Goose the Market
attractions.description: Goose the Market is a premier full-service butcher shop and gourmet specialty market located in Indianapolis' historic Fall Creek Place neighborhood (Fall Creek Place). The market features a daily rotating menu of world-class sandwiches, house-made soups, and small plates, complemented by an extensive selection of craft beer, wine, and farmstead cheeses (Goose the Market). Visitors can also enjoy the cellar Enoteca, an Italian-style wine bar offering all-ages seating and a curated drink list (Goose the Market).
attractions.address: 2503 N Delaware St, Indianapolis, IN 46205, United States (Goose the Market)
highlights: Full-service butcher counter featuring Smoking Goose house-made charcuterie and sausages (Goose the Market)
Acclaimed daily menu of gourmet sandwiches and artisan soups (Goose the Market)
Cellar Enoteca bar offering Italian-style wine by the glass and craft beers (Goose the Market)
Extensive selection of farmstead cheeses, fresh produce, and European-inspired dry goods (Yelp)
Fresh seafood catch list available for pre-order and daily pickup (Goose the Market)
Regular culinary events including pro knife sharpening and all-inclusive cooking classes (Goose the Market)

attractions.name: Harry & Izzy's Downtown Indianapolis
attractions.description: Harry & Izzy’s is an inviting, upscale American grill located in the heart of downtown Indianapolis, right next door to the iconic St. Elmo Steak House. The restaurant features a warm, Prohibition-era ambiance with a vibrant bar at its center, serving genuine Hoosier hospitality alongside premium aged steaks and fresh seafood. It offers a more modern and approachable spin on the classic steakhouse experience while maintaining a sophisticated atmosphere with dark woods and rich fabrics.
highlights: World Famous St. Elmo Shrimp Cocktail with spicy signature sauce
Premium 100% Black Angus beef aged for an average of 45 days
Signature Sliders featuring filet mignon, fried chicken, or fried shrimp
Prohibition-era ambiance with a striking two-story wine cellar (Northside) and lively circular bar
Extensive wine list and handcrafted cocktails like the Elmo Cola and Rhubarb Manhattan
Lunch and Dinner served daily with dedicated Gluten-Free menu options

attractions.name: High Velocity Sports Bar
attractions.description: High Velocity Indianapolis is a premier, high-energy sports bar that offers an elevated twist on the classic game-day experience. Located in the JW Marriott, it features over 60 HDTVs, a multi-dimensional media wall, and a menu of modern American fare, local craft beers, and hand-crafted cocktails. It is an ideal spot for fans looking to catch any game in a sophisticated yet vibrant atmosphere. (Official Website, Visit Indy)
highlights: Over 60 high-definition TVs and a massive multi-dimensional media wall for sports viewing (Official Website)
Elevated American pub fare including signature burgers, wings, and seasonal entrees (Official Website)
Extensive drink selection featuring local beers on draft and handcrafted cocktails (Visit Indy)
The "Skybox," a private dining room with a state-of-the-art sound system and 85-inch 3D screen (Indianapolis Monthly)
Daily breakfast and weekend brunch service in addition to lunch and dinner (Official Website)

attractions.name: Holliday Park
attractions.description: Holliday Park is a 94-acre urban oasis and one of Indianapolis' oldest parks, situated along the banks of the White River. It features a diverse landscape of wooded trails, a nature-inspired playground, and the iconic architectural installation known as \"The Ruins.\" (Holliday Park Foundation, Indy Parks & Recreation)
attractions.days_open: Open seven days a week (Holliday Park Foundation)
highlights: The Ruins: A unique architectural centerpiece featuring 19th-century limestone sculptures relocated from New York City. (Holliday Park Foundation)
Nature Center and Habitat Hall: A 13,000-square-foot facility with interactive exhibits, live animals, and discovery-based activities. (Holliday Park Foundation)
Hiking Trails: Over 3.5 miles of wooded trails winding through forests and ravines down to the White River. (Whimstay)
Nature-Inspired Playground: A recently renovated, inclusive play space with climbing structures and toddler-specific areas. (Holliday Park Foundation)
Arboretum and Rock Garden: A collection of over 1,200 labeled trees and a historic rock garden originally built by the WPA. (Tripadvisor)
Seasonal Events: Hosts the "Rock the Ruins" concert series in summer and a seasonal ice skating rink in winter. (Holliday Park Foundation)

attractions.name: Howl at the Moon Indianapolis (Howl at the Moon)
attractions.description: Howl at the Moon is a high-energy nightlife spot in downtown Indianapolis featuring a non-stop party with live music. Talented musicians perform everything from classic rock to today's pop hits, often on dueling pianos, in an interactive setting where the audience is encouraged to sing and dance along. The venue is known for its signature 86oz "buckets of booze" and is a popular destination for celebratory group events (Tripadvisor).
attractions.address: 20 E. Georgia St, Indianapolis, IN 46204 (Howl at the Moon)
attractions.days_open: Wednesday through Saturday (Available Sunday–Tuesday for private events) (Howl at the Moon)
highlights: Interactive dueling piano shows featuring multiple instruments (Tripadvisor)
Signature 86oz "buckets of booze" and themed cocktails (Tripadvisor)
Vibrant atmosphere with dancing and sing-alongs (Howl at the Moon)

Central location on the Georgia Street boardwalk in downtown Indy (Indianapolis Monthly)

attractions.name: IMAX Theater at the Indiana State Museum
attractions.description: The IMAX Theater at the Indiana State Museum features Indiana's largest movie screen, standing six stories tall and 84 feet wide. It provides an unparalleled immersive experience using both state-of-the-art digital projection and traditional 70mm film, showcasing everything from educational documentaries to major Hollywood blockbusters. White River State Park, Wonderful Museums
highlights: Indiana's largest movie screen at six stories tall and 84 feet wide
One of the few theaters worldwide capable of 15/70mm film projection
Immersive digital sound system custom-engineered for the auditorium acoustics
Offers both 2D and stunning 3D IMAX documentary experiences
Located in the heart of White River State Park within the Indiana State Museum
Reserved stadium seating for optimal sightlines from every seat

attractions.name: Indiana Avenue Cultural District
attractions.description: The Indiana Avenue Cultural District is a historic African American cultural hub in Indianapolis, famously known as the 'Harlem of Indy' for its thriving jazz scene from the 1920s to the 1960s. Today, it serves as a vibrant area that showcases the city's rich Black heritage, anchored by iconic landmarks and the Indianapolis Cultural Trail (Visit Indy, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 500 block of Indiana Avenue (centered at 617 Indiana Avenue)
attractions.days_open: Daily (District/Trail); Specific institutions vary (typically 5-6 days a week) (Visit Indy)
attractions.phone: (317) 236-2099 (Madam Walker Legacy Center); (317) 652-1954 (Kurt Vonnegut Museum) (Madam Walker Legacy Center, Visit Indy)
highlights: Madam Walker Legacy Center (National Historic Landmark)
Kurt Vonnegut Museum & Library
Indianapolis Cultural Trail (walking and cycling path)
Madam C.J. Walker Mural and Jazz Masters mural
Crispus Attucks High School (historic Black high school)
Lockefield Gardens (historic apartment complex)

attractions.name: Indiana Dunes National Park National Park Service
attractions.description: Indiana Dunes National Park hugs 15 miles of Lake Michigan's southern shore, offering a unique blend of towering sand dunes, quiet woodlands, and sunny prairies across 15,000 acres. The park is renowned for its exceptional biological diversity, ranking seventh among all National Park Service sites with over 1,100 native plant species. National Park Service
attractions.address: 1215 IN-49, Porter, IN 46304, USA National Park Service
highlights: Climbing towering sand dunes that rise nearly 200 feet above Lake Michigan National Park Service
Exploring 50 miles of trails through diverse ecosystems including oak savannas and marshes National Park Service
Visiting the historic Bailly Homestead and Chellberg Farm Paul in the Parks
Birdwatching for over 370 species in one of the nation's most biodiverse areas Paul in the Parks
Relaxing at West Beach, featuring lifeguards and modern facilities Paul in the Parks
Catching stunning sunsets over the lake with the Chicago skyline on the horizon Paul in the Parks

attractions.name: Indiana Fever
attractions.description: The Indiana Fever is a professional women's basketball team based in Indianapolis, competing in the Eastern Conference of the Women's National Basketball Association (WNBA). Founded in 2000, the franchise won its first WNBA Championship in 2012 and currently plays home games at the state-of-the-art Gainbridge Fieldhouse (Wikipedia, Gainbridge Fieldhouse).
attractions.days_open: Monday - Saturday (Team Store); Monday - Friday (Box Office); Game Days (Variable)
highlights: 2012 WNBA Champions and multi-time Eastern Conference title winners (Wikipedia)
Home to elite WNBA talent, including superstars like Caitlin Clark and Aliyah Boston (WNBA)
Exciting game-day atmosphere at Gainbridge Fieldhouse featuring the mascot Freddy Fever (Gainbridge Fieldhouse)
Interactive fan experiences including the Fever Inferno hip-hop dance team and the live Drumline (Gainbridge Fieldhouse)
Family-centric promotions like Lucas Oil Family Nights and the Freddy’s Kids Club (Indiana Fever)

attractions.name: Indiana Pacers NBA.com
attractions.description: The Indiana Pacers are a professional basketball team based in Indianapolis, competing in the National Basketball Association (NBA) as a member of the Eastern Conference Central Division Wikipedia. Established in 1967 as a member of the American Basketball Association (ABA), the team has a storied history including three ABA championships and a 2000 NBA Finals appearance NBA.com. They play their home games at Gainbridge Fieldhouse, a premier venue known for its basketball-first design and modern amenities Gainbridge Fieldhouse.
attractions.address: 125 South Pennsylvania Street, Indianapolis, IN 46204 Gainbridge Fieldhouse
attractions.days_open: Box Office: Monday–Friday; Venue: Open daily based on the event and game schedule Gainbridge Fieldhouse.
highlights: Home games at Gainbridge Fieldhouse, frequently cited as one of the best arenas in the NBA for basketball purists Gainbridge Fieldhouse.
The Bicentennial Unity Plaza, a massive community space outside the arena featuring public art and gathering areas Gainbridge Fieldhouse.
High-tech 'Fast Break' concession stands that use checkout-free technology for a quicker fan experience Gainbridge Fieldhouse.
Interactive game-day entertainment including the Pacers Drumline and mascot Boomer Gainbridge Fieldhouse.
Rich franchise heritage showcased throughout the arena, honoring ABA and NBA legends Wikipedia.

attractions.name: Indiana Premium Outlets
attractions.description: Indiana Premium Outlets is an open-air outlet shopping destination located approximately 35 miles south of Indianapolis. It features an exciting collection of over 70 designer and name-brand outlet stores, including Nike, Polo Ralph Lauren, and Michael Kors, offering everyday savings of up to 65% (Simon, Tripadvisor).
highlights: Over 70 designer and name-brand outlet stores (Simon)
Everyday savings of up to 65% (Simon)
Open-air shopping environment with food truck plaza (Simon)
Conveniently located off Interstate 65 at exit 76B (Simon)
Home to popular brands like Nike, Vera Bradley, and Coach (Simon)
Simon+ loyalty program for cash back and perks (Simon)

attractions.name: Indiana State Museum (Official Site)
attractions.description: The Indiana State Museum serves as the premier destination for exploring the rich heritage of Indiana, featuring three floors of interactive exhibits spanning art, science, and history (Official Site). Located in the heart of downtown Indianapolis within White River State Park, the museum showcases iconic collections such as Ice Age mastodon skeletons and the artistic 92 County Walk (Visit Indy). Visitors can engage with hands-on labs and immersive galleries that detail the state's journey from prehistoric times to modern innovations (Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 650 W Washington St, Indianapolis, IN 46204 (Official Site)
highlights: Mastodon Skeletons (including Fred the Buesching Mastodon) (Official Site)
92 County Walk (art experience featuring sculptures for every Indiana county) (Encyclopedia of Indianapolis)
IMAX Theater (Indiana's largest screen) (Visit Indy)
R.B. Annis Naturalist’s Lab (hands-on exploration of various sciences) (Official Site)
Frozen Reign (exhibit featuring cave replicas and an ice tunnel) (Tripadvisor)
Ancient Seas (fossils from Indiana's prehistoric underwater past) (Visit Indy)

attractions.name: Indiana State Soldiers and Sailors Monument
attractions.description: The Indiana State Soldiers and Sailors Monument is a neoclassical masterpiece and the physical and spiritual heart of Indianapolis. Standing at 284 feet and 6 inches, it honors Hoosier veterans who served in all wars prior to World War I, including the Revolutionary War and the Civil War. It is the first monument in the United States dedicated to the common soldier and is crowned by the iconic 30-foot bronze statue of Victory. Wikipedia), IN.gov
attractions.days_open: Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Typical schedule); Currently closed for repairs IN.gov
highlights: Massive limestone statuary groups "War" and "Peace" designed by Rudolf Schwarz IN.gov
The 30-foot-tall bronze statue of Victory (Miss Indiana) capping the obelisk Indiana War Memorials Foundation
Observation deck at 231 feet offering panoramic 360-degree views of the city IN.gov
Bronze Army and Navy astragals encircling the shaft, representing the carnage of war and naval triumphs Wikipedia)
Large cascading fountains on the east and west sides, each with a capacity of 7,000 gallons per minute IWM Brochure

attractions.name: Indiana World War Memorial
attractions.description: The Indiana War Memorial is the centerpiece of a 25-acre historic district in downtown Indianapolis, originally built to honor veterans of World War I. Modeled after the Mausoleum of Halicarnassus, the neoclassical building houses a 30,000-square-foot military museum, a 500-seat auditorium, and the breathtaking Shrine Room. (Wikipedia, IN.gov)
attractions.days_open: Wednesday–Sunday (closed Monday–Tuesday, National and State holidays except Memorial Day and Veterans Day) (IN.gov)
highlights: The Shrine Room: A vertical double cube 110 feet high, featuring 24 blood-red Vermont marble pillars and the Star of Destiny made of Swedish crystal.
Military Museum: A 30,000-square-foot space chronicling American military history from the Revolutionary War to modern conflicts.
Pro Patria: A 24-foot-high bronze sculpture by Henry Hering, once the largest cast bronze sculpture in the United States.
USS Indianapolis (CA-35) Gallery: A dedicated exhibit including a reproduction of the ship's radio room with original World War II equipment.
Colonel Eli Lilly Civil War Museum: An extensive collection of Civil War artifacts and battle flags.
Pershing Auditorium: A 500-seat venue used for civic events and ceremonies, decorated with materials from Allied nations.

attractions.name: Indianapolis 500 presented by Gainbridge
attractions.description: The Indianapolis 500, commonly known as the Indy 500, is an annual automobile race held at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway and is considered the largest single-day sporting event in the world. It features 33 drivers racing 500 miles (200 laps) on the 2.5-mile oval, serving as a cornerstone of the Triple Crown of Motorsport and a premier event in the NTT INDYCAR SERIES.
attractions.days_open: Open during the Month of May for various events; Race Day is May 24, 2026. Event Schedule
highlights: The iconic 'Yard of Bricks' finish line where winners 'Kiss the Bricks' after the race.
The traditional bottle of milk presented to the race winner in Victory Lane.
The pre-race pageantry, including 'Back Home Again in Indiana' and the release of thousands of balloons.
The Snake Pit concert venue, hosting major electronic music acts on race day.
Gasoline Alley, where fans can see the teams working on the cars in their garages.
The Borg-Warner Trophy, featuring the bas-relief faces of every race winner.

attractions.name: Indianapolis Artsgarden
attractions.description: The Indianapolis Artsgarden is a seven-story glass and steel icon suspended 17 feet over a major downtown intersection. Opened in 1995, it serves as a premier venue for arts and culture, hosting hundreds of free public performances and private events while offering 360-degree views of the city (Indy Arts Council, Wikipedia).
highlights: Suspended seven-story glass and steel dome featuring 1,962 panes of glass (Wikipedia)
Hosts over 250 free public concerts, performances, and art exhibitions annually (Indy Arts Council)
Home to the Cultural Concierge, providing local arts information, maps, and visitor guides (Visit Indy)
Connected via skywalk to the downtown hotel and office network, including the Conrad and Hyatt Regency (Wikipedia)
Offers a 360-degree panoramic view of downtown Indianapolis (Indy Arts Council)

attractions.name: Indianapolis City Market
attractions.category: Landmark, Historic Public Market, Food Hall
attractions.description: The Indianapolis City Market is a historic public landmark and food hall that has served as a community gathering place since its opening in 1886. While the main historic hall is currently closed for a major multi-year redevelopment project slated to continue until 2028, the institution continues to host the Original Farmers' Market nearby on Monument Circle. The market is also famous for the Romanesque catacombs located beneath its western plaza, which are part of the ongoing site revitalization. Visit Indy, Indianapolis City Market
attractions.days_open: Wednesday (Original Farmers' Market on Monument Circle); Main Market Hall currently closed for redevelopment Indianapolis City Market, WFYI
highlights: The Original Farmers' Market (currently on Monument Circle) Indianapolis City Market
Historic 1886 Romanesque Revival architecture Encyclopedia of Indianapolis
Extensive underground Catacombs (ruins of Tomlinson Hall) Indiana Landmarks

attractions.name: Indianapolis Colts (Wikipedia)
attractions.description: The Indianapolis Colts are a professional American football team based in Indianapolis that competes in the National Football League (NFL) as a member of the American Football Conference (AFC) South division. Since the 2008 season, the team has played its home games at Lucas Oil Stadium, a state-of-the-art multi-purpose venue known for its retractable roof and large glass windows (Wikipedia).

attractions.description: The Indianapolis Cultural Trail: A Legacy of Gene and Marilyn Glick is a world-class 8-10 mile urban bike and pedestrian path that connects six cultural districts in downtown Indianapolis. It features beautiful landscaping, public art, and provides a safe and accessible way for residents and visitors to explore the city's cultural hubs. Indianapolis Cultural Trail, Visit Indy
attractions.address: 132 W Walnut St. (Headquarters), but the trail itself is an 8-10 mile path through downtown. Indianapolis Cultural Trail, Downtown Indy

attractions.name: Indianapolis Motor Speedway
attractions.description: Constructed in 1909, the Indianapolis Motor Speedway is a premier motor racing circuit and a National Historic Landmark located in the enclave of Speedway, Indiana. It is famously known as the 'Racing Capital of the World' and is the home of the iconic Indianapolis 500 and the Brickyard 400. (Wikipedia, Official Site)
attractions.days_open: Museum: Daily (except Thanksgiving and Christmas); Grounds: Open for scheduled events and tours (IMS Museum)
highlights: Home of the Indianapolis 500, 'The Greatest Spectacle in Racing' (Wikipedia)
Designated a National Historic Landmark since 1987 (Wikipedia)
The iconic Yard of Bricks at the start-finish line (Wikipedia)
World's highest-capacity sports venue with over 250,000 permanent seats (Wikipedia)
Houses the Indianapolis Motor Speedway Museum and Hall of Fame (Official Site)
Hosts major events like the Brickyard 400 and the Sonsio Grand Prix (Official Site)

attractions.name: Indianapolis Motor Speedway Museum (IMS Museum)
attractions.description: The Indianapolis Motor Speedway Museum celebrates over a century of racing history, featuring one of the world's premier collections of racing automobiles and artifacts. Located within the historic 2.5-mile oval, it offers immersive exhibits, legendary winning cars like the Marmon Wasp, and the iconic Borg-Warner Trophy (IMS Museum).
attractions.days_open: Open daily except Thanksgiving and Christmas Day (IMS Museum)
highlights: The Borg-Warner Trophy, permanently housed at the Museum (Wikipedia)
Over 30 Indianapolis 500 winning cars, including the 1911 Marmon Wasp (Wikipedia)
The new Qualifying Zone, an immersive exhibit for testing racing skills (IMS Museum)

attractions.name: Indianapolis Zoo
attractions.description: The Indianapolis Zoo is a premier 64-acre non-profit institution located in White River State Park, recognized as the first in the U.S. to receive triple accreditation as a zoo, aquarium, and botanical garden. It is home to more than 1,400 animals of 235 species and serves as a global leader in conservation, notably through its biennial Indianapolis Prize (Wikipedia).
attractions.days_open: Open daily, except for Thanksgiving, Christmas Eve, Christmas Day, New Year's Eve, and New Year's Day (Visit Indy)
highlights: International Orangutan Center: One of the largest and most advanced orangutan habitats in North America.
Dolphin Pavilion: Home to daily presentations and the nation's only underwater dolphin viewing dome.
Oceans Biome: Features the nation's largest shark touch pool where visitors can interact with various shark species.
White River Gardens: A three-acre botanical garden and conservatory included with zoo admission.
Kangaroo Crossing: An immersive walk-through exhibit allowing close encounters with red kangaroos.
Magnificent Macaws: A seasonal experience featuring one of the largest flocks of macaws in North America soaring overhead.

attractions.name: Iozzo's Garden of Italy
attractions.description: Originally established in 1930 and re-opened in 2009, Iozzo's Garden of Italy is a historic family-owned restaurant in Indianapolis. It combines traditional family recipes with modern Italian-American cuisine in an upscale, memorable atmosphere. Iozzo's Garden of Italy

attractions.name: Irvington Historic District
attractions.description: The Irvington Historic District is a 545-acre residential neighborhood and one of the first planned suburbs in Indianapolis, founded in 1870. Named after author Washington Irving, it is renowned for its winding, romantic street layouts and diverse collection of Victorian, Queen Anne, and Craftsman architecture. The district served as the original home of Butler University and today hosts the city's largest annual Halloween festival. Encyclopedia of Indianapolis, Wikipedia)

attractions.name: Irwin Union Bank and Trust Company Building (Irwin Conference Center)
attractions.description: Designed by world-renowned architect Eero Saarinen and completed in 1954, this building revolutionized bank design by replacing traditional 'granite fortress' aesthetics with a transparent glass-walled pavilion. The structure is a National Historic Landmark set within a lush 'office in a garden' landscape designed by Dan Kiley, featuring nine distinctive concrete roof domes. It served as a model for modern bank architecture across the United States before being repurposed as a conference center.
attractions.days_open: Daily for exterior viewing; Tours typically Tuesday - Sunday (seasonal) Columbus Area Visitors Center

attractions.name: Kurt Vonnegut Museum and Library
attractions.description: The Kurt Vonnegut Museum and Library champions the literary, artistic, and cultural contributions of the late writer, artist, teacher, and Indianapolis native Kurt Vonnegut. It serves as a cultural and educational resource unique to the nation, featuring artifacts, art, and a reading room dedicated to his legacy (Kurt Vonnegut Museum and Library, Tripadvisor).

attractions.name: Lilly House at Newfields (Oldfields)
attractions.description: Lilly House at Newfields is a 26-acre National Historic Landmark and historic house museum located on the Newfields campus. The 22-room mansion, once the home of J.K. Lilly Jr., has been meticulously restored to reflect its 1930s splendor with original furnishings and decorative arts. The estate is a rare surviving example of the American Country Place Era, featuring extensive gardens designed by the renowned Olmsted Brothers (Newfields Official Site, Wikipedia).
highlights: Restored 1930s-era interiors featuring nearly 90% original furnishings belonging to the Lilly family (Newfields Official Site).
A 26-acre estate landscape designed by Percival Gallagher of the famed Olmsted Brothers firm (Visit Indy).

Status as a National Historic Landmark, designated for its significance in landscape architecture and interior design (Wikipedia).

attractions.name: Lincoln Square Pancake House
attractions.description: Lincoln Square Pancake House is a first-generation family-owned business that has been serving the Indianapolis area since 1989. The restaurant prides itself on providing guests with high-quality breakfast and lunch favorites at a fair price in a clean, professional, and friendly environment. Their extensive menu features breakfast all day, ranging from specialty pancakes and decadent French toast to hearty skillets and fresh-squeezed orange juice.

attractions.name: Mama Carolla's Old Italian Restaurant
attractions.description: Mama Carolla's Old Italian Restaurant is a premier dining destination in Indianapolis, famously housed in a historic 1920s stucco villa (Visit Indy). The restaurant is celebrated for its intimate, "old-world" atmosphere featuring multiple cozy dining rooms and a romantic outdoor garden terrace (Tripadvisor). attractions.name: MashCraft Brewing (On Delaware)
attractions.description: MashCraft Brewing was founded in 2014 with the simple mission of offering patrons high-quality, hand-crafted beer and exceptional service. Its Indianapolis location on Delaware St. features a family-friendly taproom and a dog-friendly patio, serving a variety of consistently great ales and lagers alongside a food menu. (MashCraft) (Visit Indy)
highlights: Family-friendly environment with a dedicated collection of board games (Indy with Kids)
Dog-friendly outdoor patio dining area (MashCraft)
Weekly trivia events held every Thursday (MashCraft)
Rotating tap list featuring a variety of craft ales, lagers, wine, and cider (MashCraft) (Visit Indy)
Full food menu including popular items like flatbreads (MashCraft)

attractions.name: Mass Ave Cultural Arts District Mass Ave Indy
attractions.description: The Mass Ave Cultural Arts District is a vibrant, 45-degree diagonal neighborhood in Indianapolis known for its "45 Degrees from Ordinary" spirit. It serves as a primary hub for independent boutiques, visionary public art, live theaters, and a diverse array of chef-inspired restaurants and nightlife Mass Ave Indy, Visit Indy.
attractions.address: Massachusetts Ave (from New York St to Bellefontaine St) Wikipedia
highlights: Visionary public art including the "Ann Dancing" installation Visit Indy
The historic Athenæum (Das Deutsche Haus), home to the city's oldest restaurant, The Rathskeller Wikipedia
Bottleworks District, a massive retail and food hall located at the northern end Bottleworks District
Live theater and music venues like the Old National Centre and Young Actors Theatre Wikipedia
Direct access to the Indianapolis Cultural Trail and Monon Trail for pedestrians and cyclists Mass Ave Indy
Dozens of locally-owned independent boutiques and art galleries Mass Ave Indy

attractions.name: Metazoa Brewing Company
attractions.description: Metazoa Brewing Co. is a pet-friendly, open-concept brewery in downtown Indianapolis that creates a wide range of traditional ales and quirky experimental styles. Founded in 2016, the brewery is dedicated to animal welfare, donating 5% of all its profits to animal and wildlife organizations in Indiana and beyond. (Metazoa Brewing Co.)
attractions.days_open: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday (Metazoa Brewing Co.)
highlights: Voted 2021 Brewery of the Year at the Great American Beer Festival (Metazoa Brewing Co.)
Donates 5% of profits to animal and wildlife organizations (Metazoa Brewing Co.)
On-site dog park and pet-friendly indoor taproom (Metazoa Brewing Co.)
Offers over 16 craft beer styles on tap, including traditional and experimental options (Metazoa Brewing Co.)
Features great views of the Indianapolis skyline (Metazoa Brewing Co.)
Regularly hosts events like trivia nights, live music, and pet adoptions (Visit Indy)

attractions.name: Miller House and Garden (Newfields)
attractions.description: The Miller House and Garden is a premier example of mid-century Modernist residence design, featuring a collaboration between architect Eero Saarinen, interior designer Alexander Girard, and landscape architect Dan Kiley. Commissioned by industrialist J. Irwin Miller and his wife Xenia, the home is renowned for its open, flowing layout, glass walls, and iconic conversation pit (Visit Columbus Indiana). It is a National Historic Landmark and is now owned and maintained by Newfields as a historic house museum (Wikipedia)).
highlights: The central sunken conversation pit with colorful Girard-designed pillows (Vitra).
Iconic allée of honey locust trees running along the west side of the house (Newfields).
Interior design by Alexander Girard featuring bold textiles and a massive storage wall (Studio O+A).
Architectural design by Eero Saarinen, including its flat roof and skylight grid supported by cruciform steel columns (Visit Columbus Indiana).
Geometric garden rooms designed by Dan Kiley that integrate seamlessly with the home (The Cultural Landscape Foundation).

attractions.name: NCAA Hall of Champions
attractions.description: The NCAA Hall of Champions is an interactive museum in downtown Indianapolis that honors collegiate athletes from all 24 NCAA-sanctioned sports. It features two floors of exhibits, including sports simulators, a 1930s-style retro gymnasium, and displays showcasing the history and spirit of student-athletes across three divisions. NCAA Hall of Champions, Wikipedia
attractions.days_open: Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday NCAA Hall of Champions
highlights: Interactive sports simulators for various collegiate sports
1930s retro gymnasium with a vintage basketball court
Video highlights and authentic artifacts from colleges nationwide
Trivia challenges to test sports knowledge
Displays featuring current team rankings and national championships
The 'One Shining Moment' theater experience

attractions.name: Newfields (Indianapolis Museum of Art) Newfields
attractions.description: Newfields is a 152-acre cultural campus that integrates art and nature, featuring the renowned Indianapolis Museum of Art, historic Lilly House, and extensive gardens. The campus also includes the 100-acre Virginia B. Fairbanks Art & Nature Park, offering visitors a diverse range of galleries, outdoor sculptures, and seasonal festivals Newfields, Wikipedia
highlights: Indianapolis Museum of Art (IMA) galleries containing over 54,000 works Wikipedia
The Virginia B. Fairbanks Art & Nature Park: 100 Acres Newfields
Lilly House and Gardens, a National Historic Landmark Wikipedia
Madeline Elder Greenhouse and the Beer Garden Tripadvisor
Seasonal events such as Harvest Nights and Winterlights Newfields Events
The Garden at Newfields featuring 40 acres of landscaped beauty Newfields

attractions.name: Rick's Cafe Boatyard
attractions.description: Rick’s Café Boatyard is an Indianapolis icon located on the waterfront of Eagle Creek's Reservoir, offering guests a panoramic view of the water. Best known for its fresh seafood flown in daily, prime steaks, and craft cocktails, it provides a unique island-paradise atmosphere in land-locked Indiana. The venue features expansive outdoor patios, live music, and specialized events, making it a premier destination for casual meals and private celebrations. Official Website, LinkedIn
attractions.address: 4050 Dandy Trail, Indianapolis, IN 46254 Official Website
highlights: Panoramic waterfront views of Eagle Creek Reservoir Official Website
Fresh seafood flown in daily from around the world LinkedIn
Extensive outdoor patio dining voted Indy's best YouTube
Live music and entertainment on the water WRTV Indianapolis
Signature Sunday Brunch menu Official Website
Full-service marina and private event spaces Official Website

attractions.name: Saint Joseph Brewery and Public House Official Website
attractions.category: Brewery, Restaurant, Bar Official Website, Tripadvisor
attractions.description: Saint Joseph Brewery and Public House is a unique brewpub and gastropub located within a historic 19th-century Catholic church. Established in 2015, the establishment preserves the original sanctuary's architecture, offering a one-of-a-kind dining atmosphere with award-winning craft beers and seasonal innovative food. Official Website, Visit Indy
attractions.address: 540 North College Avenue, Indianapolis, IN 46202 Official Website, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Shapiro's Delicatessen
attractions.description: Shapiro's Delicatessen is a legendary, fourth-generation Jewish deli and cafeteria that has been an Indianapolis staple since 1905. Known for its generous portions and award-winning corned beef, pastrami, and brisket, the restaurant serves authentic family recipes in a classic cafeteria-style setting.
highlights: Famous house-made corned beef and pastrami sandwiches stacked high on marbled rye bread
Authentic Jewish deli favorites including matzo ball soup, potato latkes, and chopped liver
Extensive cafeteria-style menu featuring meatloaf, beef Manhattan, and daily rotating specials
On-site bakery offering giant gourmet cookies, Arlene’s carrot cake, and homemade cheesecakes
Recognized as America’s oldest single-family owned deli with over 100 years of tradition
Conveniently located within walking distance of Lucas Oil Stadium and the Indianapolis Convention Center

attractions.name: Soldiers and Sailors Monument (Monument Circle)
attractions.description: The Soldiers and Sailors Monument is a 284-foot-tall neoclassical structure located in the center of Indianapolis at Monument Circle. Originally dedicated in 1902 to honor Indiana's Civil War veterans, it now serves as a tribute to Hoosiers who served in the Revolutionary War, War of 1812, Mexican-American War, and Spanish-American War. The monument is the largest outdoor memorial in Indiana and features a large observation deck, fountains, and the Colonel Eli Lilly Civil War Museum. (Wikipedia), Indiana War Memorials)
highlights: The "Victory" statue (Lady Victory) standing at the summit of the monument.
An observation deck offering 360-degree views of downtown Indianapolis.
The Colonel Eli Lilly Civil War Museum located in the monument's basement.
Cascading limestone fountains and pools on the east and west sides.
The annual "Circle of Lights" holiday display, which transforms the monument into a massive Christmas tree.
Intricate sculptures by Bruno Schmitz representing "War" and "Peace."

attractions.name: St. Elmo Steak House
attractions.description: Since 1902, St. Elmo Steak House has stood as a legendary cornerstone of Indianapolis, where history is written over dinner and tradition is passed through generations. Known globally for its fiery shrimp cocktail and timeless hospitality, this institution offers a classic steakhouse experience in a historic setting. (Official Website)
attractions.address: 127 S Illinois St, Indianapolis, IN 46225 (Official Website)
highlights: Oldest steakhouse in Indianapolis, operating in the same location since 1902 (Wikiwand)
World-famous for its signature spicy St. Elmo Shrimp Cocktail with freshly grated horseradish (Official Website)
Recipient of the James Beard Foundation 'America's Classics' Award (Official Website)
Located in the historic Braden Block building, a contributing structure to the Wholesale District's National Register listing (Wikiwand)
Features the 1933 Lounge, a sophisticated second-floor cocktail bar and speakeasy (Official Website)

attractions.name: Story Inn
attractions.category: Bed & Breakfast, Restaurant, Bar, Historic Landmark
attractions.description: Founded in 1851, Story Inn is a historic 19th-century village transformed into a premier boutique bed and breakfast and gourmet dining destination. The "distraction-free" inn offers unique accommodations in original town cottages and rooms above the general store, emphasizing a tranquil escape without televisions or phones. Story Inn About Us, Indy's Child
attractions.days_open: Daily for lodging; Restaurant and Tavern open Thursday through Sunday (with private/limited service Monday-Wednesday). Story Inn
highlights: Original 1851 General Store housing the main restaurant
The "Blue Lady" ghost, a legendary resident of the Garden Room
Story Still Tavern located in the historic basement with craft cocktails and beer
"Distraction-free" lodging with no televisions, radios, or phones in rooms
Garden-to-table seasonal cuisine featuring ingredients from the on-site culinary garden
Located in the heart of the Story Historic District, a preserved 19th-century village

attractions.name: Strange Bird
attractions.description: Equal parts neighborhood watering hole and tropical escape, Strange Bird is a cozy shotgun-style bar nestled in the historic Irvington neighborhood. It features ornate tropical cocktails and fresh shellfish platters alongside a unique menu of house-made Japanese Ramen and hand-pleated dumplings. The interior is themed as a tropical hideaway with bamboo, banana bark mats, and vibrant murals.
highlights: Ornate tropical cocktails and an extensive selection of rums
Fresh oyster bar and glistening shellfish platters
Authentic Japanese Ramen with noodles made in-house and bones simmered for hours
Themed tropical interior featuring bamboo and bac-bac matting
Regular educational classes focused on oysters and rum appreciation
Menu includes distinctive dishes like hand-pleated dumplings and crispy Katsu curry

attractions.name: Sun King Brewery
attractions.description: The original downtown Indianapolis location opened as a small twelve-tap tasting room and fifteen-barrel brewhouse in an unused warehouse space. Today, it is an award-winning production brewery and all-ages campus occupying three buildings in the heart of the city. Visitors can enjoy a wide range of core and seasonal craft beers along with food from on-site partners. Sun King Brewery, Visit Indy
highlights: Award-winning beer lineup including Sunlight Cream Ale and Wee Mac Scottish-style Ale Sun King Brewery
Large tasting room featuring over 30 rotating taps Sun King Brewery
On-site dining provided by Nacho by La Margarita Sun King Brewery
