Private event space available in the inviting Barrel Hall Sun King Brewery
Family-friendly environment welcoming guests of all ages Sun King Brewery

attractions.name: T.C. Steele State Historic Site
attractions.description: Nestled in the rolling hills of Brown County, this 211-acre site was the home and studio of renowned Hoosier impressionist T.C. Steele and his wife Selma. Visitors can explore the House of the Singing Winds, Steele’s "Large Studio" filled with his paintings, and beautifully restored gardens and hiking trails that inspired his masterpieces (Indiana State Museum, Wikipedia).
highlights: House of the Singing Winds (preserved home)
The Large Studio (filled with paintings)
Selma N. Steele Nature Preserve (92 acres)
Restored formal gardens
Five hiking trails
Steele Memorial Cemetery (Indiana State Museum)

attractions.name: Taxman CityWay Gastropub
attractions.description: Taxman CityWay is a family-friendly gastropub and beer garden located in a historic 1850s livery building in downtown Indianapolis. It specializes in Belgian-inspired craft beers paired with a seasonal farm-to-table menu featuring locally sourced ingredients. (Taxman Brewing Co., Visit Indy)
highlights: Located in a renovated 1850s livery building with rustic aesthetics.
Offers 20 craft beers on draft, specializing in Belgian-inspired styles.
Features a seasonal farm-to-table menu with local ingredients and a Belgian twist.
Spacious, dog-friendly outdoor beer garden and patio.
Family-friendly dining areas along with a dedicated 21+ bar.
Prime downtown location next to Gainbridge Fieldhouse, ideal for pre-event dining.

attractions.name: The Children's Museum of Indianapolis
attractions.description: The Children's Museum of Indianapolis is the world's largest museum of its kind, featuring a massive 482,950-square-foot campus dedicated to interactive family learning. The facility spans five floors of indoor exhibits covering dinosaurs, space exploration, and world cultures, complemented by the 7.5-acre outdoor Riley Children's Health Sports Legends Experience. As a non-profit institution, it aims to ignite joy, wonder, and curiosity through immersive, hands-on experiences for children and adults alike. Official Website, Life In Indy
attractions.days_open: Tuesday–Sunday (Open Mondays March–Labor Day) Official Website
highlights: Dinosphere: An immersive exhibit featuring real dinosaur fossils and interactive prehistoric environments. Official Website
Riley Children's Health Sports Legends Experience: 7.5 acres of outdoor sports activities including basketball, football, and golf. Visit Indy
Fireworks of Glass: A stunning 43-foot-tall permanent glass sculpture by artist Dale Chihuly. Official Website
The Carousel: A National Historic Landmark originally built in 1917. Expedia
Beyond Spaceship Earth: An exhibit exploring the International Space Station and the future of space travel. Official Website
Corteva Agriscience ScienceWorks: Hands-on exploration of biology, geology, and physical science. Official Website

attractions.name: The Historic Brown County Art Gallery
attractions.description: The Historic Brown County Art Gallery, established in 1926, is one of the oldest art galleries in the United States and serves as a cornerstone of the Brown County Art Colony. Its 15,000-square-foot facility houses both an extensive permanent collection of historic Indiana art and contemporary works by current Association members. The gallery provides a comprehensive look at the region's artistic heritage through rotating exhibits, workshops, and educational programs. (Official Website, Wikipedia)
highlights: The Gustave Baumann Indiana Collection of woodblock prints
The Wm. Zimmerman Studio featuring meticulous bird illustrations
Glen Cooper Henshaw Permanent Collection with over 80 rotating works
15,000 square feet of exhibition space including a working art studio
Rotating exhibits from the 45-member Brown County Art Gallery Artist Association
Home to the annual Indiana Heritage Arts juried competition and exhibition

attractions.name: The Penrod Arts Fair (CBIZ Penrod Arts Fair)
attractions.description: The Penrod Arts Fair, known as 'Indiana’s Nicest Day,' is one of the largest single-day art fairs in the country. Held on the beautiful campus of Newfields since 1967, it features over 350 artists, multiple performance stages, a children’s area, and local food and beverages. Penrod Arts Fair, Discover Newfields
attractions.days_open: One day only (the first Saturday after Labor Day)
attractions.phone: No public phone number listed; contact via info@penrod.org or penrod@dittoepr.com Penrod Arts Fair Contact
highlights: Over 350 juried artist booths spanning four distinct areas.
Five stages of live entertainment including dance, jazz, blues, and symphony.
Dedicated Children’s Area with family-friendly activities and performances.
Showcases more than 50 arts-related non-profit organizations and project booths.
Held on the historic 152-acre Newfields campus (formerly Indianapolis Museum of Art).

attractions.name: The Rathskeller at the Athenaeum The Rathskeller
attractions.category: Restaurant, Bar, Entertainment Venue The Rathskeller
attractions.description: Set in the historic, 19th-century Athenaeum Building downtown, The Rathskeller is reminiscent of both a quaint inn tucked in the Bavarian hills and a lively beer hall in Munich. As Indianapolis' oldest restaurant, it has been in operation since 1894 and is regularly voted the city's best German restaurant, offering a blend of authentic Germanic fare and contemporary cuisine The Rathskeller, Visit Indy
highlights: Oldest restaurant in Indianapolis, established in 1894 Athenaeum Foundation
Housed in the historic Athenaeum, a 19th-century German cultural center The Rathskeller

attractions.name: The Slippery Noodle Inn
attractions.category: Bar, Restaurant, Blues Club, Historic Landmark
attractions.description: The Slippery Noodle Inn is Indiana's oldest continuously operated bar in its original building, having opened in 1850 as the Tremont House. Listed on the National Register of Historic Places, it is a legendary downtown destination known for hosting live blues music seven nights a week across two performance stages. (The Slippery Noodle, Wikipedia)

Listed on the National Register of Historic Places since 1982.

attractions.name: The Workingman's Friend
attractions.description: The Workingman's Friend is a legendary, family-owned American diner and dive bar in Indianapolis with a history spanning over 100 years. Established in 1918, it is renowned for its massive "smash" burgers, crisp onion rings, and unpretentious, no-frills atmosphere. The establishment remains a local institution, consistently recognized by national publications for serving some of the best burgers in the state. Wikipedia, Official Website
highlights: Award-winning "smash" burgers and crispy onion rings Visit Indy
Historic atmosphere dating back to its founding in 1918 Wikipedia
Cash-only service maintaining its classic dive bar feel Official Website

Full bar featuring draft beer and "schooners" of Bud Light Official Website
Authentic "seat-yourself" experience popular with locals and visitors alike Official Website

attractions.name: Tinker Street Restaurant
attractions.description: Tinker Street is an upscale, 21-and-over urban cottage restaurant known for its seasonal, locally sourced menu and inviting atmosphere. The establishment emphasizes fresh ingredients from Indiana farms and offers a diverse selection of plant-based and meat-centric dishes paired with a curated wine list.
highlights: Seasonal, frequently changing menu with many locally sourced ingredients from Indiana farms.
Complimentary 'Tinker toast' (sparkling wine) served to guests upon arrival.
Extensive selection of plant-based, vegetarian, and vegan-friendly dishes alongside meat options.
Curated international wine list and a small selection of classic cocktails.
Cozy urban cottage atmosphere with both indoor and outdoor seating options.
Strictly a 21-and-over establishment, ensuring an adult-oriented dining experience.

attractions.name: Town of Nashville (Village of Nashville)
attractions.description: The Village of Nashville, Indiana, is a world-renowned arts colony and the quintessential heart of Brown County. Known for its rustic charm and artistic heritage, the downtown area features over 200 unique boutiques, craft studios, and galleries housed in historic buildings. It serves as a vibrant hub for tourism, offering a blend of fine dining, local music, and artisanal craftsmanship against a backdrop of scenic rolling hills.
attractions.address: 211 South Van Buren Street, Nashville, IN 47448
highlights: Over 200 unique specialty shops and art galleries
Historic Brown County Art Colony heritage
Pioneer Village with authentic 19th-century buildings
Live entertainment at the Brown County Playhouse and Music Center
Local wineries, distilleries, and craft breweries offering tastings
Scenic walking tours and historic architecture throughout the village

attractions.name: Town of Speedway
attractions.description: Established in 1926, the Town of Speedway is a unique enclave of Indianapolis that serves as the global hub for racing culture. It is world-renowned as the home of the Indianapolis Motor Speedway and the iconic Indianapolis 500 race. The neighborhood features a vibrant, revitalized Main Street filled with local breweries, restaurants, and shops, offering a small-town feel just minutes from downtown Indianapolis. (Visit Indy, Town of Speedway)
highlights: Indianapolis Motor Speedway: The legendary 2.5-mile track known as "The Brickyard" and home to the Indy 500. (Visit Indy)
Revitalized Main Street: A walkable corridor featuring local favorites like Big Woods Speedway and Daredevil Brewing. (Town of Speedway)
Indianapolis Motor Speedway Museum: A facility dedicated to the history of the track and automobile racing. (IMS Museum)
Dallara IndyCar Factory: An interactive museum and venue where visitors can learn about the technology of IndyCars. (Dallara Experience Hub)
Meadowood and Leonard Parks: Well-maintained community spaces offering walking trails, playgrounds, and picnic shelters. (Town of Speedway)
Speedway Indoor Karting: A world-class indoor karting facility providing racing experiences for all skill levels. (SIK Indy)

attractions.name: Turkey Run State Park
attractions.description: Turkey Run State Park is Indiana's second state park, renowned for its ancient sandstone gorges and scenic ravines carved by glacial meltwater. Visitors can explore deep canyons, old-growth forests, and the historic Lusk Home while enjoying activities along Sugar Creek. Indiana DNR
highlights: Hike through deep sandstone ravines on rugged trails like Trail 3 and 9.
Cross the iconic 202-foot suspension bridge over Sugar Creek.
Visit the historic Salmon Lusk Home and Mill site dating back to 1841.
Explore the Rocky Hollow-Falls Canyon Nature Preserve and the Richard Lieber Log Cabin.
Enjoy canoeing, kayaking, and tubing on the scenic Sugar Creek.
Participate in planetarium programs and interactive exhibits at the Nature Center.

attractions.name: Upland Brewing Company Fountain Square
attractions.description: Upland Brewing Company's Fountain Square location features a small-batch brewery for innovative barrel-aging and 22 beers on tap. The venue includes a locally-focused menu of spirited pub classics and vegetarian cuisine, along with an immense dog-friendly patio. It also uniquely houses an on-site bike shop operated by Gray Goat Bicycle Co. (Upland Brewing Co)
highlights: Small-batch brewery focused on innovation and barrel-aging
22 craft beers available on tap
Locally-focused menu with extensive vegetarian options
On-site full-service bike shop (Gray Goat Bicycle Co.)

Directly accessible via the Indianapolis Cultural Trail

attractions.name: Victory Field
attractions.description: Victory Field is a premier minor league ballpark in downtown Indianapolis and home to the Indianapolis Indians, the Triple-A affiliate of the Pittsburgh Pirates. Opened in 1996, the stadium is renowned for its scenic views of the city skyline and family-friendly atmosphere. It features 12,230 permanent seats along with a popular outfield lawn that accommodates an additional 2,000 fans. MiLB.com, White River State Park
attractions.days_open: Daily April–September for games; Box Office and Gift Shop open Monday–Friday year-round. MiLB.com
highlights: Home of the Indianapolis Indians Triple-A baseball team MiLB.com

attractions.name: Vida
attractions.description: Vida embodies a modern expression of culinary technique with worldly influence, all set within a relaxed yet refined ambiance. As one of the few Indianapolis restaurants to earn AAA's coveted Four Diamond Award, it offers an exceptional fine-dining experience centered on fresh, innovative cuisine.
attractions.address: 601 E New York St, Indianapolis, IN 46202
highlights: Only Indianapolis restaurant to earn AAA's Four Diamond award.
James Beard Award-nominated culinary team led by Chef Thomas Melvin.

attractions.name: Virginia B. Fairbanks Art & Nature Park (100 Acres)
attractions.description: The Virginia B. Fairbanks Art & Nature Park is a 100-acre urban oasis and interactive sculpture park situated on the Newfields campus in Indianapolis. It features site-specific contemporary art installations set within a diverse landscape of woodlands, wetlands, a 35-acre lake, and a native pollinator meadow, emphasizing the reciprocal relationship between art and the environment. Wikipedia, Official Site
highlights: Funky Bones: A popular climbable fiberglass sculpture by Atelier Van Lieshout Wikipedia
Stratum Pier: A multi-layered steel and fiberglass pier by Kendall Buster Official Site

attractions.name: White River Gardens Indianapolis Zoo
attractions.description: White River Gardens is a 3.3-acre botanical oasis located within White River State Park and integrated into the Indianapolis Zoo's main entrance. The facility features the 5,000-square-foot glass Hilbert Conservatory and the outdoor DeHaan Tiergarten, offering a serene blend of lush tropical plants, themed gardens, and whimsical art. Indianapolis Zoo, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 1200 W. Washington St., Indianapolis, IN 46222 Indianapolis Zoo
highlights: Hilbert Conservatory: A 5,000-square-foot glass-enclosed tropical garden Indianapolis Zoo

attractions.name: White River State Park
attractions.description: White River State Park is a premier 250-acre urban park located in the heart of downtown Indianapolis, Indiana. It stands as the only cultural urban state park in the United States, offering a unique blend of green spaces, trails, and world-class attractions. Visitors can explore various museums, the Indianapolis Zoo, and a scenic canal walk, all situated along the picturesque banks of the White River. (Wikipedia), (White River State Park)
highlights: Indianapolis Zoo & White River Gardens
Eiteljorg Museum of American Indians and Western Art
Indiana State Museum & IMAX Theater
NCAA Hall of Champions
Victory Field (Home of the Indianapolis Indians)
Everwise Amphitheater at White River State Park

attractions.name: Yats Cajun Creole Restaurant
attractions.description: Yats is a local New Orleans style joint in Indianapolis that offers a rotating daily menu of seven to ten Cajun and Creole-inspired sauces and stews served over rice with buttered baguettes (Yats Story). The atmosphere is straightforward and eclectic, featuring counter service and a laid-back, community-focused vibe that has made it a local staple (Visit Indy).

Every entree served over rice with a signature slice of toasted Cajun buttered baguette (Yats Story)

attractions.name: Zaharakos Ice Cream Parlor and Museum
attractions.description: Zaharakos is a historic ice cream parlor and museum in Columbus, Indiana, that has been a local landmark since 1900. It offers visitors a step back in time with its magnificent 1908 Welte orchestrion, a vast collection of pre-1900 soda fountains, and authentic Victorian-era decor. Beyond its famous sundaes and ice cream, the parlor serves classic American fare like the Gom Cheese-brr-grr in an elegantly restored setting. Official Website, Wikipedia
highlights: 1908 Welte Orchestrion (self-playing organ) that sounds like a full orchestra
Largest collection of pre-1900 soda fountains on public display in the museum area
The Crystal Parlor, a magnificently restored Victorian-era suite on the second floor
Historic 50-foot mahogany backbar featuring stained glass and mirrors from 1911
Soda Fountain and Mechanical Music Libraries containing thousands of historical documents
